---
title: "IL Fundamentals"
---

Imitation learning in this repository trains policies from demonstration data rather than reward optimization. The IL stack uses LeRobot ACT and Diffusion policies on Azure ML and OSMO.

## Core Concepts

| Concept | Practical meaning in this toolchain |
| --- | --- |
| Demonstration | Recorded episode containing synchronized observations and actions |
| Behavioral cloning | Supervised learning from observation-action pairs |
| Policy architecture | ACT or Diffusion policy selected at submission time |
| Generalization | Policy behavior on unseen episode states |
| Data quality | Coverage, consistency, and sensor/action integrity |

## Why IL Here

| Benefit | Relevance |
| --- | --- |
| Fast bootstrap | Policies can be trained with task demonstrations before reward engineering |
| Operator alignment | Policies learn behavior that reflects expert operation style |
| Practical pipeline fit | Existing data flow supports LeRobot dataset structure |

## Policy Types

| Policy | Strength | Typical tradeoff |
| --- | --- | --- |
| ACT | Strong temporal coherence for manipulation trajectories | Sensitive to dataset bias and out-of-distribution states |
| Diffusion | Better multi-modal action modeling | Higher runtime complexity |

## Data Requirements

| Dimension | Requirement |
| --- | --- |
| Coverage | Include representative starts, disturbances, and completion states |
| Consistency | Align timestamps across image, state, and action channels |
| Format | LeRobot-compatible structure with valid `meta/info.json` and episode files |
| Scale | Enough episodes to cover task variability, not only nominal runs |

## Typical Failure Modes

| Failure mode | Observable signal | Initial mitigation |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Covariate shift | Error compounds over rollout horizon | Add corrective demonstrations and expand state diversity |
| Label noise | High loss floor and unstable validation trends | Filter bad episodes and revalidate transforms |
| Narrow behavior cloning | Strong results on seen trajectories only | Expand dataset diversity and evaluate across scenario splits |

## Next Steps

- [LeRobot Training](../training/lerobot-training.qmd)
- [Preparing Datasets for Training](../recipes/preparing-datasets.qmd)
- [LeRobot ACT Policy Inference](../evaluation/lerobot-evaluation.qmd)
